summary: Registered PVF dispatcher, direct publicVariable channels, BattlEye posture and server-side authority gaps.

summary: Client dialog resources, HUD/title resources, menu controller loops, marker/action UI and legacy WASP overlay wiring.

summary: PVF dispatch accepts a sender-chosen command string and executes it through Call Compile, making the dispatcher a major live-server trust boundary.
nextAction: Replace dynamic compile dispatch with an allow-listed handler map and keep direct PV channels in scope.

summary: The repo ships only a minimal BattlEye publicVariable rule for kickAFK and no server.cfg/basic.cfg/scripts.txt hardening bundle.
nextAction: Ask owner for production BEpath/server config; do not count BattlEye as shipped mitigation until supplied.

summary: AI supply truck autonomy is config-gated latent breakage: UpdateSupplyTruck compile is commented, the live spawn remains, and the referenced supplytruck.fsm is missing.
nextAction: Either restore a complete FSM-backed truck owner or replace with the PR #1 helicopter/cash-run model.

summary: MASH respawn can exist while MASH map marker synchronization is broken because the server emits WFBE_SE_MASH_MARKER_SENT but the client receiver compile is commented.
nextAction: Register or delete the marker receiver path; keep respawn feature status separate from marker UI status.

summary: Legacy RscMenu_Upgrade points to missing Client/GUI/GUI_Menu_Upgrade.sqf; live upgrade UI uses WFBE_UpgradeMenu and GUI_UpgradeMenu.sqf.

summary: The wiki maps major wait gates, but a complete table of condition, producer, timeout/logging and failure symptom is still pending.

summary: Generated-output drift and CI checks are recognized needs; repo currently lacks an automated parity check lane for docs/code changes.
nextAction: Generate parity inventory and decide whether to add CI for LoadoutManager/build output drift.

summary: On master, supply missions are truck-based, client-started and server-completed: the client writes SupplyFromTown/SupplyAmount on a vehicle and the server trusts those vars after command-center proximity checks.
nextAction: For hardening, move eligibility, payload amount and reward calculation to server-owned state.

summary: Supply mission cooldown has a casing mismatch: town init seeds lastSupplyMissionRun, while server supply mission code reads/writes LastSupplyMissionRun.
nextAction: Normalize casing or prove current variable behavior harmless in Arma 2 OA before relying on cooldown semantics.

summary: PR #1 adds supply helicopter tiers, SupplyByHeli, air/cash/interdiction rewards and buy-menu highlighting on top of the existing object-var supply mission flow; it does not implement autonomous AI-flown logistics.
nextAction: Review PR #1 as an additive player-run supply extension; keep AI logistics as separate deferred work.

summary: PR #1 adds a Killed event handler on every supply mission start without a guard/removal, so reused vehicles can accumulate handlers; double pay is currently muted because the handler clears SupplyAmount.
nextAction: Before merging PR #1, add an object-var EH guard or remove/reuse the previous handler.

summary: Town AI inactivity cleanup can delete a town-AI vehicle containing a player passenger/crew member when the player is not the group leader, because server_town_ai.sqf deletes wfbe_active_vehicles based on !(isPlayer leader group _x) and does not inspect crew/cargo/turret occupants.
nextAction: Use Town-AI-Vehicle-Despawn-Safety.md to patch server_town_ai.sqf with a full player occupancy guard while preserving empty AI-only cleanup.

summary: agent-hardening-backlog.jsonl is the machine-readable backlog for future Codex/Claude/code-owner work packages, complementing Hardening-Implementation-Roadmap.md and including both implementation-ready and verification-ready tasks.
nextAction: Future agents should claim work packages from agent-hardening-backlog.jsonl instead of scraping roadmap prose.

summary: Arma-2-OA-External-Reference-Guide.md maps official BI OA scripting references to Wasp hotspots for multiplayer/JIP, public variables, PVEHs, setVariable, event-handler stacking, nearestObjects, compile/preprocess diagnostics, render/simulation scope and performance timing.
nextAction: Use this guide before accepting Arma 3-era assumptions in future Wasp mission patches.

summary: HC delegation documentation now has split ownership: AI-Headless-And-Performance.md is the runtime/source router, Lifecycle-Wait-Chain.md owns HC boot timing and wait-chain risk, and Headless-Delegation-And-Failover-Playbook.md owns DR-21/DR-42 update-back, work-record, disconnect and late-HC policy.

summary: Hardening authority documentation now has split ownership: Hardening-Implementation-Roadmap.md is the canonical patch-order hub for priorities, branches and validation gates, while Server-Authority-Migration-Map.md is the design/checklist/table page for authority migrations. Focused playbooks own exact patch shape.
nextAction: Future authority docs/code work should update the roadmap only for sequence/validation changes, the authority map for reusable design/checklist changes, and focused playbooks for implementation details.

summary: ICBM-Authority-Playbook.md is the canonical DR-27 implementation guide. The client Tactical menu has local affordance checks, but a forged RequestSpecial "ICBM" payload can bypass UI and reach Server_HandleSpecial.sqf, which currently trusts payload side/team/objects before spawning NukeDammage. Server patching should validate commander/team, side, module/upgrade, funds/cost, impact anchor and idempotency before launch acceptance.
nextAction: Future code owner should implement hardening/icbm-authority from ICBM-Authority-Playbook.md and keep roadmap/status pages as short routing summaries.

summary: External-Integrations.md is the canonical shipped BattlEye/server-filter posture page. It owns the in-tree publicvariable.txt AFK rule evidence, missing broader filter/config bundle, and production BEpath owner caveat from DR-30. Feature Status, Networking/PV, Hardening roadmap and Server authority map should route there instead of repeating the evidence.
nextAction: Future BattlEye/public-server hardening docs should update External-Integrations.md first, then leave short routing notes on subsystem and authority pages.

summary: Deep-Review-Findings.md DR-11 and DR-36 are the canonical victory/endgame correctness evidence. DR-11 owns winner-inversion impact; DR-36 owns the server_victory_threeway.sqf:23 guard/precedence mechanism, no-break side loop, and clean Perf/JIP review. Server runtime, Hardening roadmap and Feature Status should route there instead of repeating the full mechanism.
nextAction: Future victory/endgame docs or patches should update victory-endgame-correctness in agent-hardening-backlog.jsonl and DR-11/DR-36 first, then keep other pages as short routing summaries.

summary: Lifecycle-Wait-Chain.md is the canonical page for lifecycle flags, boot ordering, JIP waits and HC wait hazards. Mission-Entrypoints-And-Lifecycle.md owns include graph and role-dispatch orientation, SQF-Code-Atlas.md owns compile/source ownership, and Server-Gameplay-Runtime-Atlas.md owns long-running server loop owners.

Economy, Server runtime, Feature Status and Hardening roadmap should route to those pages instead of duplicating mismatch evidence.

Construction-And-CoIn-Systems-Atlas.md owns construction runtime flow, request-handler map and safe extension checklist. Server-Authority-Migration-Map.md owns migration design. summary: SEND_MESSAGE direct publicVariable handler call-compiles network text (Client_onEventHandler_SEND_MESSAGE.sqf:27, multi-language branch) — a second client-side RCE independent of the PVF dispatcher; DR-1 assumed no other network-data compile sites. Common_SendMessage.sqf:26 shares the sender-local pattern.
nextAction: Codex: cross-link DR-46 and correct DR-1 single-compile-site note; code owner: change the multi-language branch to localize+format of a [STR_key,args] array and patch the identical Common_SendMessage.sqf line.

summary: remoteexec.txt is Arma 3-only (A2 OA has no remoteExec); BattlEye filter files are processed locally by the server-side BE component independent of BE's (defunct) online support for A2. updateclient.sqf:153-162 kickAFK is repo evidence the deployment relies on BE publicvariable.txt. BattlEye = contingent defense-in-depth; server-authority is the primary fix.
nextAction: Codex: drop remoteexec.txt from A2 missing-filter lists, name the A2 set, reframe BE as defense-in-depth in External-Integrations.

summary: Supply-Mission-Authority-Cleanup-Playbook documents a draft refactor as if it were current source. Nonexistent in source: a Killed event handler on the supply vehicle (0 grep hits for addEventHandler/Killed across Server|Client/Module/supplyMission), a 'SupplyByHeli' object var (0 grep hits anywhere), a heavy-heli cash-run + commander-team-funds completion branch, and recommended-patch constants WFBE_C_SUPPLY_TRUCK_TYPES/WFBE_C_SUPPLY_HELI_TYPES_T2/T3. Current source: client stamps SupplyFromTown (supplyMissionStart.sqf:20) + SupplyAmount (:34); completion (supplyMissionCompleted.sqf, 36 ln) reads those two and calls only ChangeSideSupply (:26) - supply only, no funds path. Scan at supplyMissionStarted.sqf:28 is nearestObjects [...,[],80] (empty filter); the DR-39 narrowing to ['Base_WarfareBUAVterminal'] is NOT applied, yet the page status table says 'Source/Vanilla claimed-patched'. Page also self-contradicts (Step 5 shows [], findings table claims narrowed).
nextAction: Codex: mark the playbook clearly as a PROPOSED PR (not applied) or correct each step to current source; remove SupplyByHeli/Killed-handler/heavy-heli/funds claims; fix the 'patched' status for the scan; replace nonexistent constants with the inline hardcoded type arrays.

summary: ICBM-Authority-Playbook sentence 'Client Tactical-menu checks are affordances; server-side validation remains the authority boundary' reads as a factual claim that server validation exists. Server_HandleSpecial.sqf ICBM case (:97-112) takes _side/_base/_target/_playerTeam from the request args, logs, then only `if (isNull _target || !alive _target) exitWith {}` (timing/null gate) and spawns NukeDammage - no commander-identity, upgrade-level, or funds validation. Fully client-authoritative, matching DR-27. The sentence should be normative ('should remain'), not present-tense factual.
nextAction: Codex: reword the authority-boundary sentence to normative/aspirational and link DR-27 inline so a reader does not assume server validation already exists.

summary: Function-And-Module-Index lists Common/Module/Reaktiv as 'Live'. Reaktiv_Init.sqf is never called from Init_Common.sqf or any init file - grep for Reaktiv references outside Common/Module/Reaktiv/ returns zero. The module's two files (Reaktiv_Init.sqf, Reaktiv_OnHandleDamage.sqf) reference only each other; the Init is never invoked, so the module is dead/unreachable as shipped. Contrast IRS, which IS compiled at Init_Common.sqf:320.
nextAction: Codex: change Reaktiv status from 'Live' to dead/unreachable (Init never called) on Function-And-Module-Index; consider noting it in Abandoned-Feature-Revival-Review.

summary: Core-Systems-Index 'Administrative/Operational Systems' lists 'Discord bot status publishing' alongside genuine in-mission systems (anti-stack, AFK-kick via BattlEye publicvariable filter, server-FPS publication, performance-audit logs, global-game-stats extension export). No Discord publishing code exists in the mission SQF - grep finds only plain-text mentions (Init_Client.sqf:959 community-link hint, briefing.sqf diary, stringtable.xml teamkill message). Discord publishing is the separate DiscordBot/ C# repo component, which consumes the stats the mission already exports via the extension (the 'global game stats extension export' item). Listing it as a mission operational system is a scope/double-count error.
nextAction: Codex: remove 'Discord bot status publishing' from the in-mission operational systems list, or relabel it an external DiscordBot/ integration that consumes the extension stats export.

summary: Factory-And-Purchase-Systems-Atlas labels WFBE_C_TOWNS_PURCHASE_RANGE (60) as the depot purchase range used by updateavailableactions.fsm. The FSM actually gates depot availability with _tcr = WFBE_C_TOWNS_CAPTURE_RANGE (40): line 39 reads CAPTURE_RANGE into _tcr, line 194 does depotInRange = !isNull([vehicle player, _tcr] call WFBE_CL_FNC_GetClosestDepot). PURCHASE_RANGE (60) is used only by GUI_Menu_BuyUnits.sqf for the factory dropdown list. Two different constants with different defaults (40 vs 60), so the depot in-range gate is tighter than the page implies. Constants at Init_CommonConstants.sqf:323 (CAPTURE=40) and :338 (PURCHASE=60).
nextAction: Codex: correct the FSM depot-range row to WFBE_C_TOWNS_CAPTURE_RANGE (40); note PURCHASE_RANGE (60) is the GUI dropdown range only.

summary: Systemic wiki issue: several remediation/playbook pages describe the post-fix TARGET state and label it 'source/Vanilla claimed-patched' or 'current source', though the fix is unmerged or unimplemented. Verified branch landscape (git ls-remote origin): only master, feat/supply-helicopter (PR #1, open, commit 1faf738d), docs/developer-wiki-claude, docs/developer-wiki-index. DR-19 (FPS publisher !isDedicated early-exit) and DR-39 (supply-scan nearestObjects class-narrowing) are implemented on NO branch. PR #1 adds supply helicopters (SupplyByHeli, Killed-EH, cash-run, WFBE_C_SUPPLY_*_TYPES constants) but does NOT narrow the scan. Affected pages: Hosted-Server-FPS-Loop-Sleep, Supply-Mission-Scan-Narrowing, Performance-Opportunity-Sweep, Supply-Mission-Authority-Cleanup-Playbook. A reader on master cannot distinguish proposed-state from shipped-state.
sourceRefs: git ls-remote origin (4 branches: master, feat/supply-helicopter, docs/developer-wiki-claude, docs/developer-wiki-index)
nextAction: Codex: adopt a Status convention (Shipped(master) / Proposed(PR #N) / Proposed(no branch)) across remediation pages; remove false 'patched/current source' labels; add a PR-vs-master banner to PR1-derived content.

summary: DR-19 (hosted/listen FPS publisher busy-spin) is NOT fixed on any branch. serverFpsGUI.sqf (12 lines) and monitorServerFPS.sqf (8 lines) both run while{true} do { if(isDedicated) then { ...; sleep 8; } } with the sleep ONLY inside the isDedicated branch and NO exitWith/!isDedicated guard - so a listen/hosted server enters the loop and spins a core with no sleep. Confirmed identical on Chernarus and the generated Vanilla Takistan copies (grep for exitWith/!isDedicated across all four = 0 hits). Multiple wiki pages claim this is 'source/Vanilla claimed-patched' - false.
nextAction: Codex: mark DR-19 as Proposed (not in master); correct the publisher descriptions to the real while{true}/inside-isDedicated shape. (Note: dedicated servers are unaffected; listen/hosted only.)

summary: PR #1 (feat/supply-helicopter, open/mergeable, head 1faf738d) is REAL unmerged work that extends player supply missions from trucks to transport helicopters. It introduces (on its branch, not master): WFBE_C_SUPPLY_TRUCK_TYPES / WFBE_C_SUPPLY_HELI_TYPES_T2 / _T3 / _HELI_TYPES / _VEHICLE_TYPES constants, a SupplyByHeli end-to-end flag, a Killed event handler on the supply vehicle (with a confirmed EH-stacking defect - re-added every mission start with no guard/removal, impact bounded by SupplyAmount zeroing), heavy-heli +20% payload, and a Supply-upgrade-3 cash-run converting heli deliveries to commander-team funds (diverting the side supply pool). It does NOT add AI-flown supply helis (deferred; UpdateSupplyTruck FSM compile is commented out on master) and does NOT narrow the nearestObjects scan. The Current-Work-Supply-Helicopters-PR1 wiki page documents it accurately and is clearly labelled.
nextAction: Codex: scope PR1-derived content (Supply-Mission-Authority-Cleanup-Playbook etc.) clearly as PR #1 vs master; the Killed-EH stacking defect is a real review item for the PR.

summary: SYSTEMIC: the wiki Status field 'source/Vanilla claimed-patched' (a definitive 'fix is applied in current Chernarus + generated Vanilla Takistan source' claim - confirmed semantics via Client-Skill-Init-Idempotency lines 5/23/33/49 'the former second Skill_Init.sqf call is gone in source', validation criteria listed as passing) is FALSE on 8 DR-remediation pages whose fixes are not in master (verified vs Chernarus + Vanilla Takistan; git ls-remote shows no fix branches). Pages + the still-live bug: Hosted-Server-FPS-Loop-Sleep (DR-19 FPS busy-spin), Supply-Mission-Scan-Narrowing (DR-39 scan []), Performance-Opportunity-Sweep (DR-19/39), Supply-Mission-Authority-Cleanup-Playbook (PR1/DR-39), Client-Skill-Init-Idempotency (double Skill_Init at Init_Client.sqf:571), Commander-Reassignment-Call-Shape (DR-15 _side=_this at Server_AssignNewCommander.sqf:3 + dup notification), Factory-Queue-Counter-Token-Cleanup (DR-33 varQueu random Client_BuildUnit.sqf:167-168 + :365 no decrement), Paratrooper-Marker-Revival (DR-2 HandleParatrooperMarkerCreation unregistered, Init_PublicVariables.sqf:39). Likely cause: pages document the intended post-patch state and were marked 'patched' before the source edits landed. Clean DR-remediation pages (accurate): UI-IDD-Collision-Repair, WASP-Marker-Wait-Cleanup, Arma-2-OA-Compatibility-Audit, Abandoned-Feature-Revival-Review.
sourceRefs: git ls-remote origin (no per-DR fix branches)
nextAction: Codex/owner: reconcile the Status field across all DR-remediation pages - relabel 'Proposed / patch-ready (NOT in master)' where unmerged, or land the patches. Do not leave 'patched' on a bug still live in master.

summary: WASP-Overlay documents a non-existent self-test: it lists test/wasp_selftest.sqf as a Live file wired at init.sqf:4 (lines 29/39) with a detailed behavior section (lines 59-66: 240s WFBE_PRESENTSIDES wait, 20 samples at 30s, [WASP-SELFTEST] diag_log tag, GetAICommanderFunds/GetTownsSupply/GetCommanderTeam logging). Verified absent from master: no root init.sqf, no test/ directory, no *selftest* file (glob 0 hits), 0 grep hits for WASP-SELFTEST anywhere. The file, its wiring, and its behavior section are fabricated. Separately, the page cites the WASP_procInitComm compile at initJIPCompatible.sqf:253/:253-255; it is at :243 (commented block :241-245), and the page treats the correct :241-245 as a 'historical' observation.
sourceRefs: Missions/[55-2hc]warfarev2_073v48co.chernarus/ (no init.sqf, no test/ dir, no *selftest* file)
nextAction: Codex: remove the fabricated test/wasp_selftest.sqf section from WASP-Overlay; correct the WASP_procInitComm ref :253 -> :243 (comment block :241-245).

summary: Deep-Review-Findings is reliable overall (71/72 claims clean), but its Fix-2 PVF-allowlist remediation snippet (lines 140-141) uses the Arma-3-only `apply` array command: `WFBE_SE_PVF_ALLOWED = _serverCommandPV apply {format ["SRVFNC%1", _x]}` and the CLTFNC twin. `apply` is Arma 3 1.56+; it does NOT exist in A2 OA 1.64 (source grep finds only the English word 'apply' in comments, never the array command). The page itself declares 'Arma 2 OA 1.64 - not Arma 3' (line 5) and warns about isEqualTo (A3-only) at line 134, but missed `apply`. An A2-OA implementation must build the allowlist with a forEach loop.
nextAction: Codex: rewrite the Fix-2 allowlist snippet as an A2-OA forEach loop (no `apply`); consider adding `apply` to the Arma-2-OA-Command-Version-Reference A3-only list.

summary: CAPSTONE of the patched-but-unmerged status drift: the Progress-Dashboard (the project's rolled-up status surface) marks client-skill-init-idempotency, supply-mission-scan-narrowing, and hosted-server-fps-loop-sleep as 'Published/source-patched' though all three bugs are still live in master (Skill_Init called twice at Init_Client.sqf:547+571; supply scan still nearestObjects [...,[],80] at supplyMissionStarted.sqf:28; monitorServerFPS.sqf still busy-spins on non-dedicated). So the drift is not only on individual remediation pages - it is aggregated into the dashboard a human/agent consults for 'what is done'. CONTRAST: the Codebase-Coverage-Ledger is accurate (0/30 inconsistencies). Actionable fix: reconcile the dashboard's statuses to the ledger + master source, or adopt the Shipped(master)/Proposed convention everywhere.
nextAction: Codex/owner: reconcile the Progress-Dashboard statuses to master + the (accurate) Codebase-Coverage-Ledger; do not show live bugs as Published/source-patched.

summary: Arma-2-OA-Compatibility-Audit.md and agent-compatibility-audit.json are the canonical docs scan for accidental Arma 3 assumptions. Current docs hits for remoteExec, BIS_fnc_MP, remoteExecutedOwner, parseSimpleArray, RVExtensionArgs, CfgFunctions, CBA/ACE and Eden are intentional guardrails, explicit non-options, evidence caveats or terrain/folder-name coincidences, not implementation advice.
handoff: Future agents should load agent-compatibility-audit.json or run the audit search before adding engine-version-sensitive docs or accepting prompts that propose Arma 3-era APIs.

summary: Gear-Template-Profile-Filter.md documents a patch-ready bug: Client_UI_Gear_SaveTemplateProfile.sqf sets _template_upgrade but uses undefined _u_upgrade in weapon, magazine and backpack upgrade checks before saving WFBE_PERSISTENT_%SIDE_GEAR_TEMPLATE.
handoff: Future gear UI owner should replace _u_upgrade with item-upgrade or template-upgrade comparison in source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla, and smoke allowed/locked template persistence with no RPT undefined-variable error.

summary: Vehicle-Cargo-Equip-Loop-Bounds.md documents a confirmed patch-ready bug: Common_EquipVehicle.sqf and Common_EquipBackpack.sqf apply cargo using inclusive 0..count(_items) loops, while Client_GetVehicleContent.sqf uses the correct count(_items)-1 pattern.
handoff: Future gear UI owner should change all five equip loops to count(_items)-1 in source Chernarus, propagate Vanilla, and smoke exact cargo counts plus empty cargo groups with no RPT out-of-range errors.

summary: Service-Menu-Affordability-Guards.md documents a confirmed patch-ready local bug: GUI_Menu_Service.sqf enables actions from cached prices before recalculating, rearm/refuel debit unconditionally, and repair/heal miss fresh action-time affordability checks.
handoff: Future gear UI owner should add action-time price/funds/context guards before service debits/spawns, handle Man healing separately from vehicle _canBeUsed, propagate Vanilla, and smoke affordable/unaffordable service actions without claiming server authority.

summary: Source-Fix-Propagation-Queue.md centralizes current Chernarus-only fixes that still need Vanilla propagation and Arma smoke: paratrooper marker registration, client skill init idempotency, hosted FPS loop exits and supply mission scan narrowing.
handoff: Future release owner should run LoadoutManager from a correctly named a2waspwarfare checkout, inspect Vanilla diffs, avoid modded propagation claims, and record paratrooper/skill/FPS/supply smoke before marking source-only fixes release-complete.

summary: agent-release-readiness.json is the machine-readable companion to Source-Fix-Propagation-Queue.md. It records the current source-only fixes, generated target status, LoadoutManager path requirement, smoke gates and patch-ready-not-source-patched lanes so agents can avoid claiming release completion prematurely.
handoff: Future release/check agents should load agent-release-readiness.json before running propagation, preparing release notes or marking any source-only fix as complete.

summary: AI-Commander-Autonomy-Audit.md is the canonical revival-readiness page for AI commander autonomy. Source evidence shows state/funds and the upgrade worker exist, but no audited source owner starts the autonomous commander loop, schedules upgrades, drives AIBuyUnit or uses the move interval. Old AI supply trucks remain config-gated broken because UpdateSupplyTruck is uncompiled and calls missing supplytruck.fsm.
handoff: Future AI/autonomy owner should choose minimal guard/remove versus full revival before autonomous supply-heli or commander production work; do not just uncomment UpdateSupplyTruck.

summary: Integration-Trust-Boundary-Audit.md is the canonical security-first integration page. DiscordBot GameData.LoadFromFile uses Newtonsoft TypeNameHandling.All on database.json; the in-repo a2waspwarfare_Extension writer uses TypeNameHandling.None; AntiStack calls separate absent A2WaspDatabase wrappers and compiles returns; the shipped BattlEye filter is AFK-only.
handoff: Future integration owner should harden DiscordBot JSON intake first, make extension writes observable, add AntiStack missing-DLL/return-shape guards, and avoid claiming BattlEye hardening without production BEpath evidence.

summary: AntiStack-Database-Extension-Audit.md is the canonical runtime/procedure map for AntiStack. Current source has WFBE_C_ANTISTACK_ENABLED default-on behavior plus disabled-mode guards for loops, wrappers, first-join skill checks, disconnect and victory persistence; enabled mode still compiles A2WaspDatabase returns and assumes array shape.
handoff: Future AntiStack owner should add a central safe response validator/circuit breaker, fix REQUEST_SIDE_SKILL fallback shape, preserve disabled-mode/team-swap behavior, and smoke valid DB, missing DB and timeout modes.

summary: The Arma 2 OA compatibility audit was refreshed after recent wiki expansion. Current risky Arma 3-era terms remain intentional warnings, explicit non-options, evidence contrasts or folder/fork caveats; no incorrect Arma 3 implementation advice was found.
handoff: Future agents should update the scan snapshot when adding engine-version-sensitive terms, especially in AntiStack DB hardening, PVF/server-authority migration, lifecycle refactors, BattlEye design or UI/marker performance rewrites.

summary: Respawn-And-Death-Lifecycle-Atlas.md is the canonical source-backed page for player death cleanup, custom respawn UI, spawn-source rules, AI respawn, MASH live-vs-dead-marker split and kill scoring. It also records a patch-ready custom-gear penalty mode 5 affordability/skip edge.
handoff: Future respawn owner should patch mode 5 only after owner semantics are clear; keep local MASH respawn, MASH marker revival/removal and team-shared MASH design as separate decisions.

summary: Upgrades-And-Research-Atlas.md is the canonical source-backed map for WFBE_UP_* constants, side upgrade configs, live WFBE_UpgradeMenu/GUI_UpgradeMenu, RequestUpgrade -> Server_ProcessUpgrade, client sync, AI commander upgrade worker, stale RscMenu_Upgrade and the patch-ready server-authority migration plan.
handoff: Future upgrade owner should add server-side requester/commander/side/current-level/dependency/cost/funds validation before WFBE_SE_FNC_ProcessUpgrade and run a side-config array alignment validator before expanding upgrade indexes.

summary: Towns-Camps-And-Capture-Atlas.md is the canonical source-backed map for mission.sqm town startup, Init_TownMode/Init_Town, starting ownership modes, server_town capture/SV, global camp capture, client marker visibility, town AI activation/deactivation and town/camp capture reward authority.
handoff: Future town owner should preserve the split between capture/SV, camp capture and town AI; preserve side-scoped SV visibility state; move capture bounty/score authority server-side only as a dedicated reward-authority patch.

Future commander/HQ owner should patch DR-15 separately, then migrate normal MHQ repair and WASP cash HQ recovery to server validation without breaking HQ deploy/mobilize JIP state or allied-only wreck markers.
summary: Commander-HQ-Lifecycle-Atlas.md is the canonical source-backed page for commander vote/reassignment, client commander affordances, HQ deploy/mobilize, HQ destruction, allied wreck markers, normal MHQ repair, WASP cash HQ recovery, base-area JIP risk and HQ authority notes.

Victory-And-Endgame-Atlas.md is the canonical developer map for match-end detection, loser/winner payload semantics, client outro flow, live Server_LogGameEnd, stale PVFunctions/LogGameEnd cleanup, AntiStack final persistence and DR-11/12/13/36 patch-ready correctness risks.
handoff: Future victory owner should patch source Chernarus by computing sideEliminated/sideWonByTowns, guarding both with !WFBE_GameOver, deriving winner/loser explicitly, preserving current client loser-side payload unless updating Client_EndGame too, exiting after one winner, then propagating Vanilla and smoking elimination/all-towns/same-tick/AntiStack paths.

summary: Player-Join-Disconnect-And-AntiStack-Lifecycle.md is the canonical page for join paths, JIP/reconnect, disconnect cleanup, team identity, AntiStack persistence, raw lifecycle PVs and AFK edges.
handoff: Patch playerObjectsList indexing and disconnect cleanup before expanding reconnect-sensitive features or AntiStack lifecycle behavior.

summary: Marker-Cleanup-Restoration-Systems-Atlas.md is the canonical page for client marker families, MARKER_CREATION, HQ wreck markers, garbage/empty vehicle/mine cleanup and building restorer risks.
handoff: Patch mine pair removal and trash flag alignment first; smoke local/global marker visibility before changing marker deletion or JIP behavior.

summary: Mission-Parameters-Localization-And-Generated-Build-Inputs.md is the canonical page for Params cache flow, stringtable hazards, generated version.sqf, LoadoutManager overwrite boundaries and root/7za tooling blockers.
handoff: Fix root discovery and packaging separation before claiming generated propagation from Codex checkouts; add generated-file banners for future agents.

summary: Support-Specials-And-Tactical-Modules-Atlas.md is the canonical page for tactical support menu, RequestSpecial dispatch, paratroopers, paradrops, UAV, artillery, ICBM/Nuke, MASH, ZetaCargo, service actions and supply supports.
handoff: Validate RequestSpecial server-side before accepting support effects; fix Zeta detach and stale ICBM/MASH marker paths as smaller lanes.

summary: Chernarus source patched supplyMission/playerObjectsList.sqf by moving _i = 0 before the WFBE_SE_PLAYERLIST loop so matching UIDs update their actual row rather than index 0; Vanilla and Arma smoke are pending.
handoff: Do not call this release-complete until Vanilla propagation runs from a correctly named a2waspwarfare checkout and reconnect/JIP plus supply-completion smoke is recorded; stale UID-row removal and disconnect cleanup races remain separate hardening work.

summary: Wave H harvested Huygens and Euler. Huygens expanded supply lifecycle risks beyond the source index patch; Euler produced a compact direct-PV table that was folded into Public-Variable-Channel-Index rather than copied wholesale.
handoff: Treat source player-list indexing as patched but not release-complete; treat stale row cleanup, cooldown authority and truck-var reward validation as separate open hardening work. Direct PV channels remain outside the PVF dispatcher fix and need owner-page validation.

summary: Wave H confirmed that the next public-server hardening slice is not only PVF dispatch allowlisting; registered command payloads still need authority checks for score/kill reports, join, commander, MHQ repair, base-area accounting, RequestSpecial subcommands and HQ-kill forwarding. Respawn/HQ pages already capture MASH marker relay orphaning, respawn penalty mode 5 and MHQ repair locality, now cross-linked from the networking authority table.

Wave I harvested five narrow scouts. Economy docs now distinguish client-trusted mutation from server-derived read/award patterns; AntiStack docs now include UID/object/owner identity and disconnect persistence risk; UI docs now capture RHUD ownership and stale dialog/TODO edges; commander docs now separate live human commander systems from unproven AI autonomy; respawn/HQ docs now capture local MASH respawn versus dead marker sync and HQ marker cleanup gaps.

Spot-check reconfirmed all five current source-only fixes are present in Chernarus source and still absent from maintained Vanilla Takistan: paratrooper marker registration, duplicate Skill_Init removal, hosted FPS early exits, supply command-center scan narrowing and supply player-object list indexing. LoadoutManager was not run because this checkout path is work/a instead of a2waspwarfare.

source: Codex LoadoutManager build/run and diff inspection
summary: LoadoutManager now runs from normal repo-root marker checkouts and can skip packaging with A2WASP_SKIP_ZIP=1. A propagation run completed for Chernarus/Takistan with packaging skipped; maintained Vanilla Takistan now contains the five tracked fixes. Arma 2 OA smoke remains pending before release-complete claims.

The five current source + maintained Vanilla propagated fixes now have a concrete smoke pack: paratrooper marker PVF, client Skill_Init idempotency, hosted/dedicated server FPS loop behavior, supply command-center scan narrowing and supply player-object list indexing. These are planned runtime gates, not completed Arma 2 OA evidence.
handoff: Do not mark the propagated fixes release-complete until hosted/dedicated/JIP smoke evidence is recorded against this pack.

summary: Six scoped explorers returned and were harvested: Tesla supplied runtime smoke gates; Socrates/Gibbs corrected feature-status and abandoned-system evidence; Boyle tightened supply authority, reward and player-object lifecycle risks; Carver validated machine-file contracts; Zeno supplied navigation/footer fixes. Reports are promoted into owner pages; future agents should read docs and machine records instead of raw scout output.
handoff: Remaining gates are docs/wiki mirror parity, link/JSON validation and Arma 2 OA runtime smoke for propagated fixes.
